description: Full benchmark suite — 30 stories across 5 complexity tiers and 2 target projects

sample-ts-api.description: TypeScript Express REST API with Prisma ORM, JWT auth, and Vitest

sample-react-app.description: React 19 + TypeScript SPA with Vite, TanStack Query, and Vitest

stories.title: Fix incorrect default export in date utility
stories.description: The `src/utils/format-date.ts` module exports `formatDate` only as a named export. Several modules import it as a default import (`import formatDate from './format-date'`) and fail at runtime with `undefined is not a function`.
acceptance_criteria: formatDate is exported as both named and default export
All existing named imports continue to work
No TypeScript errors
stories.complexity_notes: Single-line addition, trivial scope

stories.title: Fix off-by-one in pagination helper
stories.description: The `paginate()` function in `src/utils/pagination.ts` returns one fewer item than requested. When `limit=10`, only 9 results are returned. The `.slice(offset, offset + limit - 1)` should be `.slice(offset, offset + limit)`.
acceptance_criteria: paginate(items, { offset: 0, limit: 10 }) returns exactly 10 items when available
paginate() with offset beyond array length returns empty array
Existing pagination test passes without modification
stories.complexity_notes: Single character fix, but requires understanding the bug from description

stories.title: Add missing return type annotations to auth helpers
stories.description: The functions in `src/auth/helpers.ts` — `hashPassword`, `comparePassword`, and `generateToken` — are missing explicit return type annotations. TypeScript infers them correctly but the project's eslint config requires explicit return types on exported functions.
acceptance_criteria: All three exported functions have explicit return type annotations
Types match the inferred types (Promise<string>, Promise<boolean>, string)
No TypeScript or ESLint errors
stories.complexity_notes: Mechanical type annotation, no logic change

stories.title: Fix broken CSS class name in navigation component
stories.description: The `NavBar` component in `src/components/NavBar.tsx` uses `className='nav-active'` but the CSS module exports it as `navActive`. The active state styling is completely missing in production.
acceptance_criteria: Active nav item displays correct styling
CSS module import is used consistently (styles.navActive)
No runtime className mismatches
stories.complexity_notes: String replacement in JSX, trivial

stories.title: Remove unused imports flagged by tree-shaking
stories.description: The build output warns about unused imports in three files: `src/routes/users.ts` imports `Request` but only uses `Router`, `src/middleware/cors.ts` imports `NextFunction` but doesn't use it, and `src/utils/logger.ts` imports `format` from `date-fns` but uses `formatISO` instead.
acceptance_criteria: All three unused imports are removed
No other imports are affected
Build completes with zero warnings about unused imports
All tests still pass
stories.complexity_notes: Three files but each is a single-line deletion

stories.title: Fix environment variable fallback in database config
stories.description: The database config in `src/config/database.ts` reads `process.env.DB_PORT` but doesn't provide a fallback, so it's `undefined` in local development. Should default to `5432`.
acceptance_criteria: DB_PORT defaults to 5432 when env var is not set
DB_PORT uses env var value when set
Type is number, not string
stories.complexity_notes: One-line fix with parseInt and fallback

stories.title: Add input validation to config parser with custom errors
stories.description: The config parser in `src/config/index.ts` uses `JSON.parse()` directly without validation. Invalid JSON causes an unhandled exception that crashes the server. Add proper error handling with custom error classes and descriptive messages.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid JSON throws ConfigParseError with descriptive message
Missing required fields (name, version) throw ConfigValidationError
Valid configs parse correctly (no regression)
Unit tests cover: invalid JSON, missing fields, empty object, valid input
stories.complexity_notes: New error classes + validation logic + tests. Multi-file but focused.

stories.title: Add request logging middleware with structured output
stories.description: Add an Express middleware that logs every request with structured JSON: method, path, status code, duration in ms, and request ID. Use the existing logger from `src/utils/logger.ts`. Skip logging for health check endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Every request (except /health) produces a structured JSON log line
Log includes: method, path, statusCode, durationMs, requestId
Request ID is a UUID attached via X-Request-Id header
Health check endpoints (/health, /ready) are excluded
Unit tests verify log output format and health exclusion
stories.complexity_notes: New middleware file, integration with existing logger, duration timing, UUID generation

stories.title: Implement soft delete for user records
stories.description: Currently user deletion is a hard DELETE from the database. Implement soft delete: add a `deletedAt` timestamp column, modify the delete endpoint to set this timestamp instead of removing the row, and filter soft-deleted users from all queries by default.
acceptance_criteria: User model has deletedAt nullable timestamp field
DELETE /users/:id sets deletedAt instead of removing row
GET /users excludes soft-deleted users by default
GET /users?includeDeleted=true returns all users
Soft-deleted users cannot log in
Tests cover: soft delete, query filtering, login rejection
stories.complexity_notes: Touches model, routes, and auth. Requires understanding query patterns.

stories.title: Add form validation with error display to login page
stories.description: The login form in `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` submits without any client-side validation. Add Zod schema validation for email format and password minimum length (8 chars). Display inline error messages below each field.
acceptance_criteria: Email field validates format (must contain @)
Password field requires minimum 8 characters
Errors display inline below the relevant field in red
Errors clear when user starts typing in that field
Form does not submit while validation errors exist
Tests verify: invalid email, short password, valid submission
stories.complexity_notes: React form state + Zod validation + conditional rendering + tests

stories.title: Fix race condition in debounced search input
stories.description: The search bar in `src/components/SearchBar.tsx` fires API requests on every keystroke with a 300ms debounce, but responses can arrive out of order — typing 'ab' then quickly 'abc' sometimes shows results for 'ab' because that response arrived last. Implement request cancellation.
acceptance_criteria: Only the most recent search request's results are displayed
Previous in-flight requests are cancelled via AbortController
Debounce still works (no request on every keystroke)
Tests verify: cancellation of stale requests, correct final result
stories.complexity_notes: Async race condition fix, AbortController, cleanup in useEffect

stories.title: Add retry with exponential backoff to HTTP client
stories.description: The API client in `src/utils/http-client.ts` makes requests with no retry logic. Transient 5xx errors and network timeouts cause immediate failures. Add configurable retry with exponential backoff and jitter.
acceptance_criteria: Retries on 5xx status codes and network errors
Does NOT retry on 4xx status codes
Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s (configurable base)
Random jitter added to prevent thundering herd
Max retries configurable (default 3)
Tests cover: retry on 500, no retry on 400, max retries exceeded, success after retry
stories.complexity_notes: Algorithm implementation with async/await, timing, and randomness

stories.title: Extract shared Button component from duplicate implementations
stories.description: Three pages (LoginPage, SettingsPage, DashboardPage) each have inline button styling with slightly different implementations. Extract a shared `Button` component with variants: primary, secondary, danger. Include size props (sm, md, lg) and loading state.
acceptance_criteria: Shared Button component with variant prop (primary, secondary, danger)
Size prop (sm, md, lg) with appropriate padding/font scaling
Loading state shows spinner and disables click
All three pages updated to use shared Button
Visual output unchanged for existing buttons
Tests for: each variant, each size, loading state, click handler
stories.complexity_notes: Extraction refactor touching 4 existing files + 1 new component

stories.title: Add Zod schema validation to all API route handlers
stories.description: The API routes in `src/routes/` accept request bodies without validation. Add Zod schemas for the create and update endpoints of users and posts. Return 400 with structured error messages on validation failure.
acceptance_criteria: Zod schemas defined for: createUser, updateUser, createPost, updatePost
Invalid requests return 400 with { errors: [{ field, message }] } format
Valid requests pass through unchanged
Tests cover: missing required fields, wrong types, valid payloads
stories.complexity_notes: Multiple schemas + route integration. Repetitive but requires consistency.

stories.title: Add rate limiting middleware with sliding window algorithm
stories.description: Implement rate limiting middleware for the Express API using a sliding window counter algorithm. Support per-route and per-user configuration. Store counters in-memory with a Map (Redis integration later). Include /health endpoint bypass.
acceptance_criteria: RateLimiter middleware accepts config: windowMs, maxRequests, keyGenerator
Sliding window algorithm tracks request counts accurately
Returns 429 with Retry-After header when limit exceeded
X-RateLimit-Limit and X-RateLimit-Remaining headers on every response
Health endpoint (/health, /ready) excluded from rate limiting
Per-user limiting uses authenticated user ID as key, per-IP for anonymous
Stale entries are cleaned up periodically (no memory leak)
Unit tests cover: limit enforcement, window sliding, header values, cleanup
stories.complexity_notes: Algorithm implementation, timer-based cleanup, header management, multiple key strategies

stories.title: Implement JWT refresh token rotation
stories.description: The current auth system uses only access tokens. Add refresh token support: issue a refresh token alongside the access token on login, implement a /auth/refresh endpoint that rotates the refresh token, and add a token family tracking system to detect reuse of old tokens (indicating theft).
acceptance_criteria: Login returns both accessToken (15min) and refreshToken (7d)
POST /auth/refresh accepts refreshToken, returns new pair
Old refresh token is invalidated after rotation
Token family tracking: reuse of revoked token invalidates entire family
Refresh tokens stored in database with family ID
Integration tests: login flow, refresh flow, token theft detection
Unit tests: token generation, family tracking, expiry
stories.complexity_notes: Security-critical feature. Token rotation, family tracking, database model, multiple test layers.

stories.title: Build data table component with sorting, filtering, and pagination
stories.description: Create a reusable `DataTable` component that accepts column definitions and data. Support client-side sorting (click column header), text filtering (search input), and pagination (configurable page size). Use generics so it works with any data shape.
acceptance_criteria: DataTable<T> accepts columns: { key, header, sortable?, filterable? }[] and data: T[]
Clicking sortable column header sorts asc/desc with indicator
Filter input narrows results across all filterable columns
Pagination controls: prev/next, page size selector (10/25/50)
Empty state shown when no data or no filter matches
Accessible: proper aria labels, keyboard navigation for sort/page
Tests: sorting, filtering, pagination, empty state, keyboard nav
stories.complexity_notes: Generic component, multiple interactive features, accessibility, comprehensive testing

stories.title: Add WebSocket support for real-time notifications
stories.description: Add a WebSocket server alongside the Express HTTP server. Authenticated users should receive real-time notifications when: a new post is created, a comment is added to their post, or their account settings change. Include connection management and heartbeat.
acceptance_criteria: WebSocket server runs on same port as HTTP (upgrade handler)
Connection requires valid JWT (reject unauthorized)
Notification types: post.created, comment.added, settings.changed
Users only receive notifications relevant to them
Heartbeat ping/pong every 30s, disconnect stale clients
Connection manager tracks active connections per user
Integration tests: connect, authenticate, receive notification, heartbeat
stories.complexity_notes: New subsystem (WebSocket), auth integration, connection lifecycle, event routing

stories.title: Implement optimistic updates for todo list with rollback
stories.description: The todo list page makes API calls and waits for the response before updating the UI, causing visible lag. Implement optimistic updates: immediately update the UI on toggle/delete/create, then rollback if the API call fails. Use TanStack Query's mutation callbacks.
acceptance_criteria: Toggle todo: UI updates immediately, reverts on API failure
Delete todo: item removed from list immediately, restored on failure
Create todo: appears in list immediately with temp ID, replaced with real ID on success
Error toast shown on rollback with retry option
Loading skeleton shown only on initial page load, not mutations
Tests: optimistic create, optimistic toggle, rollback on failure, retry
stories.complexity_notes: TanStack Query optimistic mutation pattern, rollback logic, temp ID management

stories.title: Add comprehensive error boundary with error reporting
stories.description: The React app has no error boundaries — any component crash shows a blank white screen. Implement a layered error boundary system: a global boundary that catches everything, route-level boundaries for graceful page recovery, and an error reporting service that logs to the API.
acceptance_criteria: Global ErrorBoundary wraps the app root
Route-level boundaries wrap each page independently
Global fallback shows friendly error page with 'Reload' button
Route-level fallback shows error within the layout (nav still visible)
Error details sent to POST /api/errors with stack, component, route
Development mode shows full stack trace, production shows friendly message
Tests: component crash caught, error reported, reload works, route isolation
stories.complexity_notes: React class component (error boundaries), two layers, API integration, dev vs prod modes

stories.title: Implement file upload with streaming and progress tracking
stories.description: Add a file upload endpoint that accepts multipart form data, streams the file to disk (not buffering in memory), validates file type and size, and returns upload progress events via Server-Sent Events. Support image files up to 10MB.
acceptance_criteria: POST /uploads accepts multipart/form-data with a 'file' field
Files streamed to disk, not buffered in memory
Validates: max size 10MB, allowed types (jpg, png, gif, webp)
Returns 400 with descriptive error for invalid files
GET /uploads/:id/progress returns SSE stream with progress percentage
Upload metadata stored in database (filename, size, mimetype, path)
Tests: successful upload, oversized file, invalid type, progress events
stories.complexity_notes: Streaming, SSE, file validation, multipart parsing, multiple new files

stories.title: Add dark mode with system preference detection and persistence
stories.description: Add dark mode support to the React app. Detect system preference via `prefers-color-scheme`, allow manual toggle that persists in localStorage, and ensure all components respect the theme. Use CSS custom properties for theming.
acceptance_criteria: Theme context provides current theme and toggle function
System preference is detected on first visit
Manual toggle overrides system preference and persists in localStorage
CSS custom properties change for all color tokens (bg, text, border, accent)
No flash of wrong theme on page load (SSR-safe or blocking script)
Theme toggle button in header with sun/moon icon
Tests: default detection, toggle, persistence, CSS variable application
stories.complexity_notes: Context provider, localStorage, CSS custom properties, media query detection, FOUC prevention

stories.title: Implement event sourcing for order management
stories.description: Refactor the order management module from CRUD to event sourcing. Orders should be built from a sequence of domain events (OrderCreated, ItemAdded, ItemRemoved, OrderSubmitted, OrderCancelled). Add event store, aggregate root, and projection for read model. Existing API endpoints must remain backward compatible.
acceptance_criteria: EventStore interface with append() and getEvents() methods
InMemoryEventStore implementation for testing
Order aggregate root rebuilds state from event stream
All mutations go through domain events (no direct state modification)
OrderProjection builds read-optimized view from event stream
Existing API endpoints work unchanged (backward compatible)
Event schema validated with Zod before storage
Snapshot support for aggregates with many events (configurable threshold)
Full test coverage: aggregate rebuilding, projection consistency, concurrent events
Integration test verifies end-to-end order lifecycle through API
stories.complexity_notes: Architectural refactor touching 11+ files. Event sourcing pattern, aggregate roots, CQRS, snapshot optimization. Must maintain backward compatibility.

stories.title: Build role-based access control (RBAC) system
stories.description: Implement a flexible RBAC system. Define roles (admin, editor, viewer) with granular permissions (users.read, users.write, posts.read, posts.write, etc.). Add middleware that checks permissions on each route. Support role inheritance (admin inherits editor permissions). Include a management API for role CRUD.
acceptance_criteria: Permission model: role has many permissions, user has many roles
Built-in roles: admin (all permissions), editor (content CRUD), viewer (read-only)
Admin inherits all editor permissions, editor inherits viewer permissions
authorize('posts.write') middleware rejects unauthorized requests with 403
Management API: CRUD for roles and role-permission assignments
Only admin can modify roles
Permission check is O(1) via cached permission set per user
Database migration for roles and permissions tables
Tests: permission check, role inheritance, middleware rejection, cache invalidation
stories.complexity_notes: Cross-cutting concern touching auth, middleware, models, routes. Role inheritance graph, permission caching, migration.

stories.title: Implement full-text search with indexing and ranking
stories.description: Add full-text search across posts and comments. Build an inverted index that updates on content changes, implement TF-IDF ranking, support phrase queries and field boosting (title matches rank higher than body). Include search API with pagination and highlighting.
acceptance_criteria: SearchIndex class with index(), remove(), and search() methods
Inverted index stored in-memory with term → document mapping
TF-IDF scoring for result ranking
Phrase queries: "exact phrase" matches consecutive terms
Field boosting: title matches weighted 3x higher than body
Search results include highlighted snippets with matched terms
GET /search?q=term&page=1&limit=10 API endpoint
Index updates automatically when posts/comments are created/updated/deleted
Tests: indexing, TF-IDF ranking, phrase search, boosting, highlighting, pagination
stories.complexity_notes: Algorithm-heavy: inverted index, TF-IDF, tokenization, phrase matching. 11 files, multiple subsystems.

stories.title: Build state machine for multi-step form wizard
stories.description: Create a generic finite state machine library and use it to build a multi-step onboarding wizard (Profile → Preferences → Plan → Confirmation). Each step has its own validation, navigation guards, and data persistence. Support back/forward navigation and resume from last completed step.
acceptance_criteria: StateMachine<S, E> generic class with transition(), can(), and current() methods
Transition guards: can reject transitions based on state validation
Side effects: onEnter/onExit callbacks for each state
Wizard has 4 steps with forward/back navigation
Each step validates before allowing forward navigation
Progress persisted to localStorage, resumes on page reload
Step indicator shows completed/current/upcoming steps
Final submission collects all step data into single API call
Tests: state machine transitions, guards, wizard navigation, persistence, submission
stories.complexity_notes: Generic library + concrete usage. State machine theory, transition guards, React state coordination, persistence.

stories.title: Add database migration system with up/down and versioning
stories.description: Build a migration system that manages database schema changes. Migrations are numbered SQL files with up and down functions. Support running all pending migrations, rolling back the last N migrations, and tracking which migrations have been applied. Include a CLI wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: Migration files: NNNN_description.ts with up() and down() methods
migrations table tracks applied migrations with timestamps
migrate:up runs all pending migrations in order
migrate:down reverts last N migrations (default 1) in reverse order
migrate:status shows applied and pending migrations
Migrations run in a transaction (rollback on failure)
Lock mechanism prevents concurrent migration runs
CLI script: npx tsx scripts/migrate.ts [up|down|status]
Tests: up migration, down migration, ordering, failure rollback, lock
stories.complexity_notes: Infrastructure tooling. Transaction management, file ordering, locking, CLI. Requires careful error handling.

stories.title: Build plugin system with hot-reload and dependency resolution
stories.description: Design and implement a plugin system that allows third-party extensions to register commands, hooks, and middleware. Plugins declare dependencies on other plugins. Support hot-reload during development. Include a plugin registry, dependency resolver with cycle detection, lifecycle manager, and sandboxed execution. Build an example plugin that demonstrates all extension points.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin manifest schema (name, version, dependencies, exports) validated with Zod
PluginRegistry with register(), unregister(), get(), and list() methods
DependencyResolver detects cycles and computes correct load order (topological sort)
PluginLifecycle manages init→start→stop→destroy transitions with guards
Hot-reload via chokidar: detects file changes, reloads affected plugin and dependents
Plugins register commands via a CommandRegistry API
Plugins register hooks for plugin lifecycle events (onLoad, onUnload, onError)
Plugins add Express middleware scoped to specific route prefixes
vm2 sandbox prevents plugins from accessing process, fs, or host internals
Plugin errors caught and logged without crashing host application
Full test suite: registry CRUD, dependency resolution, lifecycle transitions, hot-reload
Example plugin demonstrating: command, hook, middleware, dependency declaration
Architecture documentation with component interaction diagram
stories.complexity_notes: Full system design with 17 files. Architectural decisions: sandbox strategy, dependency graph algorithm (topological sort), file watcher debouncing. Edge cases: circular deps, crash isolation, partial reload, dependency chain invalidation.

stories.title: Implement real-time collaborative editing with CRDT
stories.description: Build a collaborative text editor where multiple users can edit the same document simultaneously. Use a CRDT (Conflict-free Replicated Data Type) — specifically a Yjs-inspired sequence CRDT — to merge concurrent edits without conflicts. Include WebSocket transport, cursor presence, and operational history.
acceptance_criteria: CRDT implementation: insert and delete operations that commute
Document model preserves user intent on concurrent edits
WebSocket room system: users join document rooms
Cursor presence: see other users' cursor positions in real-time
Operational history: undo/redo per user (not global)
Offline support: buffer operations, sync on reconnect
Conflict resolution is automatic (no manual merge)
React editor component with real-time updates
Server persists document state on disconnect/interval
Tests: concurrent insert/delete, cursor sync, offline buffer, undo/redo, persistence
Load test: verify correctness with 5 simulated concurrent users
stories.complexity_notes: Research-level challenge: CRDT implementation, WebSocket rooms, cursor sync, operational transform history. 15+ files across algorithm, transport, and UI layers.

stories.title: Build end-to-end CI/CD pipeline with canary deployments
stories.description: Design and implement a CI/CD pipeline system within the API. It should accept pipeline definitions (YAML-like config), execute stages sequentially or in parallel, support canary deployments (route 10% traffic to new version, monitor error rate, auto-rollback if threshold exceeded). Include a dashboard page showing pipeline status.
acceptance_criteria: Pipeline config schema: stages with steps, parallel groups, conditions
PipelineRunner executes stages in defined order with parallel support
Each step has: name, command, timeout, retryCount, onFailure (abort/continue)
Canary deployment stage: routes configurable % of traffic to new version
Health monitor: checks error rate during canary window, auto-rollback if >5%
Pipeline status API: GET /pipelines/:id with stage-by-stage progress
Dashboard page: list pipelines, view current stage, see logs, trigger rollback
Pipeline events emitted via WebSocket for real-time dashboard updates
Audit log: all pipeline actions recorded with timestamps and actor
Tests: pipeline execution, parallel stages, canary routing, rollback trigger, dashboard render
Example pipeline config for the sample app itself
stories.complexity_notes: Full system: config parsing, execution engine, traffic routing, health monitoring, WebSocket events, audit trail. 14+ files spanning backend, real-time, and (optionally) frontend dashboard.
